metadata.archetype_justification: Zappa is a CLI tool and library with no persistent database connections, no user-specific state, no write-back endpoints, and no deployed API surface of its own. All operations are stateless command executions (package, deploy, update, rollback) that orchestrate AWS resources on behalf of users. Classified as stateless-utility.
metadata.context: Python framework for deploying WSGI apps on AWS Lambda.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — Zappa is a CLI tool published to PyPI with no deployed workload
top_gaps.impact: Cannot evaluate cloud-native compute readiness; structural absence rather than deficiency

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: 0% IaC coverage for the project's own operational infrastructure (GitHub settings, secrets, branch protections)
top_gaps.impact: No auditable, reproducible definition of repository-level infrastructure

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, subnet, or security group configuration — no deployed network infrastructure
top_gaps.impact: Structural; if hosted as a service, a VPC-based design would be required

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith: core.py (3,866 LOC) god class and cli.py (3,655 LOC) intertwined with no module boundaries
top_gaps.impact: Limits independent testability, maintainability, and future extraction of handler runtime

top_gaps.title: Application Security Pipeline
top_gaps.gap: No SAST, dependency scanning, or container scanning in CI/CD (no Dependabot, pip-audit, Bandit, or Semgrep)
top_gaps.impact: Dependency and code vulnerabilities may reach published PyPI releases undetected

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (extensive README, CHANGELOG, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md, example/ directory with detailed usage guides)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Index Zappa's documentation to answer developer questions about zappa_settings.json configuration, deployment patterns, and version migration between releases
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Convert README sections into structured chunks; generate embeddings for the settings reference and event source documentation. preferences.prefer includes 'bedrock' — Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases is the recommended target

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 4) — mature GitHub Actions CI and manual CD workflows
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Trigger CI runs, check build status across the Python 3.9-3.14 matrix, monitor Coveralls coverage reports, and manage the manual CD workflow (tagged releases to PyPI via OIDC trusted publisher)
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API access to the agent; define trigger conditions for the manual CD workflow dispatch

decomposition_strategy.current_state: Single Python package with tightly coupled modules. core.py (3,866 LOC god class handling Lambda, API Gateway, S3, CloudFormation, IAM, VPC, certificates) and cli.py (3,655 LOC) are heavily intertwined with no internal package structure beyond a flat module layout.

approaches.name: Strangler Fig (Parallel Track)
approaches.description: Extract handler.py + wsgi.py + asgi.py as a standalone lightweight package first (Lambda runtime only), then progressively extract AWS service modules from core.py into focused modules (lambda_ops, apigateway_ops, cloudformation_ops, s3_ops).

approaches.name: Conditional / Adaptive
approaches.description: Keep the monolithic package but refactor core.py into internal modules with clear interfaces. Don't split into separate packages unless specific need arises.

approaches.name: Big-Bang Rewrite
approaches.description: Rewrite as multi-package monorepo. Not recommended — high risk of breaking the existing user base.

evaluations.reason: Surface-gated: has_persistent_data_store=false — Zappa has no database
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — no multi-step workflows exist
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — synchronous CLI/HTTP is correct design
Surface-gated: no persistent state to back up
Surface-gated: has_deployed_workload=false — no workload requiring HA
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — sync is correct design
Archetype-N/A: stateless-utility — no operations exceed 30s in critical path
Surface-gated: has_iac_provisioning_aws_resources=false — no account-level IaC in this repo
Surface-gated: has_at_rest_data_surface=false — no data-at-rest surface owned by this repo
Surface-gated: no user-facing API or persistent data store for which SLOs are meaningful
Surface-gated: has_deployed_workload=false — deployment strategy cannot be assessed from CLI source alone

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure
findings.description: Zappa is a CLI tool and Python library published to PyPI with no deployed compute infrastructure of its own. No Lambda functions, no ECS tasks, and no EC2 instances run this tool as a service. It executes on developer machines and in GitHub-hosted CI runners.
findings.gap: No managed compute — all execution happens locally or in ephemeral CI environments. No workload to modernize in-place.
findings.recommendation: If a hosted deployment service (Zappa-as-a-Service) is desired, ECS/Fargate or Lambda are appropriate targets. For the current CLI tool use case, this gap is structural.

findings.title: No network security configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation configuration exists in this repo. Zappa is a CLI tool executed on developer machines — it has no deployed network infrastructure.
findings.gap: No network security configuration — the tool itself is not deployed in a VPC.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. If Zappa were hosted as a service, deployment into private subnets with least-privilege security groups and VPC endpoints would be required.

findings.title: No API entry point
findings.description: Zappa exposes no HTTP API of its own. It is CLI-only, invoked via `zappa deploy/update/rollback` commands. No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront serves as an entry point for the tool. (Zappa creates API Gateways for user apps, but does not expose one itself.)
findings.gap: No API entry point — tool is CLI-only.
findings.recommendation: If a programmatic API is desired in the future, expose deployment operations via API Gateway with authentication, throttling, and request validation. preferences.prefer includes 'api-gateway'.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling — Zappa has no deployed compute to scale. It runs as a local CLI process.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling — no workload to scale.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. No action needed.

findings.title: No IaC for own infrastructure
findings.description: No IaC defines Zappa's own operational infrastructure. GitHub Actions workflows exist as YAML, but no Terraform, CDK, or CloudFormation manages the project's PyPI publishing, GitHub environments, secrets, or branch protections. (Note: Zappa generates CloudFormation for user apps via troposphere, but has no IaC for itself.)
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage for the project's own infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Consider Terraform GitHub provider to codify repository settings, branch protection rules, and environment/secret management. This provides auditability and reproducibility.

findings.title: Tightly-coupled monolith
findings.description: Single Python package with no module boundaries. core.py (3,866 LOC) is a god class handling Lambda management, API Gateway setup, S3 uploads, IAM roles, CloudFormation stacks, VPC configuration, and certificate management. cli.py (3,655 LOC) directly instantiates and orchestrates core.py with pervasive cross-module coupling.
findings.gap: No module boundaries, no clear interfaces, god class pattern in core.py. Single deployable unit with no separation of concerns.
findings.recommendation: Refactor into bounded modules: extract the Lambda handler runtime (handler.py + wsgi.py + asgi.py) as an independent package; extract AWS service adapters from core.py into focused modules (lambda_ops, apigateway_ops, cloudformation_ops, s3_ops); define clean interfaces between CLI and orchestration layers. See Decomposition Strategy section.

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: No API versioning strategy — Zappa exposes no HTTP API. The CLI uses semantic versioning for PyPI releases, but zappa_settings.json has no explicit schema version field; changes are handled implicitly via deprecation warnings in code.
findings.gap: No API versioning; settings schema evolution relies on implicit deprecation warnings.
findings.recommendation: Add a settings schema version field to zappa_settings.json to enable forward-compatible configuration parsing. Document schema evolution rules in the README.

findings.title: Environment-variable endpoint configuration only
findings.description: Environment variables are used for AWS endpoint configuration via boto3 (AWS_DEFAULT_REGION, AWS_PROFILE, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID). boto3 handles AWS service endpoint resolution internally. No hard-coded AWS endpoints in the codebase, but no dynamic service discovery mechanism either.
findings.gap: No dynamic service discovery. boto3's endpoint resolution is adequate for a CLI tool making AWS SDK calls, but the code is not set up to use an external service registry.
findings.recommendation: No action needed for the CLI use case — boto3's endpoint resolution is the correct pattern for AWS SDK-based tools.

findings.title: S3 usage without parsing pipeline
findings.description: Zappa uses S3 for deployment package storage (ZIP uploads) but has no parsing or extraction pipeline. Deployment packages are opaque ZIP archives, not parsed or indexed for content discovery.
findings.gap: Data in S3 but no automated parsing or extraction pipeline.
findings.recommendation: For the deployment tool use case, no parsing pipeline is needed — ZIPs are consumed by Lambda directly. If deployment analytics are desired (package size trends, dependency analysis), consider S3 event-driven processing via EventBridge.

findings.title: No API authentication
findings.description: No API authentication — Zappa is a CLI tool with no HTTP endpoints. It does not expose an API that requires per-request authentication. Authentication to AWS is handled by the boto3 credential chain (environment variables, AWS profiles, IAM roles).
findings.gap: No API authentication — no API surface exists to protect.
findings.recommendation: If Zappa were to expose a programmatic API (deployment-as-a-service), implement OAuth2/JWT authentication backed by Cognito or a centralized IdP. For the current CLI tool use case, the boto3 credential chain is the correct pattern.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No centralized identity integration. Zappa relies on the AWS IAM credential chain (environment variables, ~/.aws/credentials, instance profiles) for authentication to AWS services. No Cognito, Okta, or other IdP integration for tool-level access control.
findings.gap: No centralized identity — relies entirely on local AWS credential configuration.
findings.recommendation: For a CLI tool, the AWS IAM credential chain with SSO support (aws sso login, IAM Identity Center) is the standard pattern. Document AWS SSO/IAM Identity Center integration in the README.

findings.title: Credentials in environment variables without rotation
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in source. AWS credentials are handled via the boto3 credential chain; PyPI publishing uses GitHub OIDC trusted publisher (no long-lived tokens). However, no AWS Secrets Manager integration and no rotation policy is documented.
findings.gap: Production credentials in environment variables without a documented rotation policy. No Secrets Manager integration.
findings.recommendation: Document credential rotation best practices in the README. Encourage IAM roles and SSO over long-lived access keys. If programmatic deployment use cases arise, add AWS Secrets Manager support for credential retrieval.

findings.title: No compute hardening or patching
findings.description: No compute hardening configuration — Zappa has no deployed compute to harden. The tool runs on developer machines and on GitHub-hosted CI runners (auto-patched by GitHub).
findings.gap: No compute hardening — no deployed workload to harden.
findings.recommendation: For CI, GitHub-hosted runners are auto-patched. For the package itself, add dependency vulnerability scanning (see SEC-Q7).

findings.title: No application security pipeline
findings.description: No SAST, DAST, or dependency vulnerability scanning in CI/CD. The pipeline runs lint checks (flake8, black, isort) but no security-focused scanning. No Dependabot, pip-audit, Safety, Bandit, or Semgrep.
findings.gap: No security scanning — zero security validation steps in the pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for dependency updates. Integrate pip-audit or Safety for vulnerability detection. Add Bandit or Semgrep for Python SAST. Configure blocking gates on critical findings. Quick, high-value addition given the mature CI/CD (INF-Q11=4).

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No tracing instrumented in the Zappa CLI tool itself. While Zappa can configure X-Ray for user-deployed Lambda apps, the tool has no tracing for its own deployment operations — CloudFormation polling, S3 uploads, IAM updates are not traced.
findings.gap: No tracing of deployment operations. Complex multi-step deployments have no end-to-end trace visibility.
findings.recommendation: Consider OpenTelemetry instrumentation for core.py to trace deployment operations and identify AWS API bottlenecks. Low value unless deployment troubleshooting becomes a pain point.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: No custom metrics published. Deployment success rates, package sizes, and deployment durations are not tracked.
findings.gap: No business metrics — deployment operations are not measured or tracked.
findings.recommendation: Consider publishing anonymized deployment telemetry (success/failure rate, duration, package size) to inform tool improvement priorities. Opt-in with clear disclosure.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No alerting configured — Zappa has no deployed service to monitor.
findings.gap: No alerting — no production service to alert on.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. If deployment telemetry is added (see OPS-Q3), alerting on failure-rate spikes would become valuable.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, automated remediation, or structured incident workflows in the repo. Troubleshooting is ad hoc through GitHub Issues.
findings.gap: No runbooks or incident response automation.
findings.recommendation: Add structured troubleshooting guides for common deployment failures (CloudFormation rollback, Lambda size limits, IAM permission errors) to docs/. Convert to machine-readable form for future agent consumption.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No observability ownership defined. No CODEOWNERS for monitoring assets, no dashboards, no team-attributed alarms.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — no production service with monitoring needs.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for a CLI tool. If Zappa were deployed as a hosted service, define CODEOWNERS for observability assets and named alarm owners.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No resource tagging governance for own infrastructure (none exists). Zappa supports user-configurable tags for user-deployed resources via zappa_settings.json but has no tagged AWS resources of its own.
findings.gap: No tagging governance — no AWS resources owned by this repo.
findings.recommendation: No action needed for the tool itself. Document tagging best practices for users configuring zappa_settings.json tags_to_resources (cost allocation, ownership, environment).

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=1 (primary — monolith), INF-Q1=1 (supporting — no managed compute)

triggering_questions.note: Tightly-coupled monolith with god class pattern in core.py (3,866 LOC)
No managed compute — CLI tool with no deployed workload

immediate_actions: Extract handler.py + wsgi.py + asgi.py as a standalone lightweight package
Define clean interfaces between CLI, orchestration, and handler layers
Split core.py into focused AWS service adapter modules

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 meets threshold, but contextual guard blocks: no deployed workload exists (CLI tool, not a running service)

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold but contextual guard blocks: no EC2/VM workload to containerize

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Zappa is a CLI tool published to PyPI. There is no running service to containerize — compute is Lambda-based when users deploy their own apps via Zappa. The contextual guard 'must be EC2/VM-based' prevents this pathway from triggering.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); no commercial database engines detected

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures — trigger condition requires < 3

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No database, no stored procedures, no commercial engines detected. DATA-Q4=4 does not meet the < 3 threshold.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 Not Evaluated (surface-gated — no database); DATA-Q3=4 (no database to version)
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No database exists in this repository. INF-Q2 was Not Evaluated due to surface gating (has_persistent_data_store=false).

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A for stateless-utility); no data processing workloads exist
pathways.not_triggered_reason: INF-Q4 was Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A for stateless-utility — sync is correct). No streaming, ETL, or data processing workloads exist in this repository.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (primary — no IaC for own infrastructure); SEC-Q7=1 (supporting — no security pipeline)

triggering_questions.note: No IaC defines project's own operational infrastructure

immediate_actions: Add Dependabot for dependency vulnerability scanning
Add Bandit or Semgrep for Python SAST in the CI workflow
Consider Terraform GitHub provider to codify repo settings, branch protections, and secrets

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context 'Python framework for deploying WSGI apps on AWS Lambda' contains no AI-related signal terms (AI, agent, LLM, Bedrock, GenAI, RAG, vector, embedding, etc.). Note: preferences.prefer includes 'bedrock', but preferences steer recommendation framing only and do not trigger pathways per Constraint C6.
